top_tweets.text:
Pretty telling how Anthropic 

1. Thinks it’s perfectly acceptable to ban a 60-person paying org without justification

2. Are comfortable outsourcing this to some automated system

3. Do no human review nor offer human contact

4. Get it wrong and customer now super pissed
top_tweets.author: GergelyOrosz
top_tweets.name: Gergely Orosz
top_tweets.summary_zh: Anthropic 在未給理由的情況下直接封禁一家 60 人付費組織帳號，全交由自動化系統處理且無人工審查管道，顯示出對企業客戶支援的輕忽態度。

top_tweets.text: there are likely ~100 ppl alive today that deeply understand all of these together: product instinct, what makes good software, design, technical depth, a real model of ai, the psychology of a single user, the shape of culture, team building, the ability to motivate, & the
top_tweets.author: signulll
top_tweets.name: signüll
top_tweets.summary_zh: 當今真正同時深刻理解產品直覺、優質軟體、設計、技術深度、AI 模型、使用者心理、文化走向、團隊建設與激勵能力的人，全世界大概只有約 100 人。

top_tweets.text:
The AI debate is difficult because one side views art as a product to be delivered as fast as possible. 

It's like going to a lake and telling fisherman that it's "more efficient" to buy fish from the grocery store. They aren't just there for the fish.
top_tweets.author: sean_gause
top_tweets.name: Sean // Hear Tell of Hauntings🥀
top_tweets.summary_zh: AI 辯論的核心分歧在於一方把藝術當成要快速交付的產品；但就像釣魚不只是為了魚，創作過程本身也是價值所在。

top_tweets.text:
product management tip: ragebaiting your user base/player base by making inaccurate claims about the quality of your systems is an incredibly fast way of identifying current problems with your software that you can then prioritize fixes for

easily the top reason I’m on here
top_tweets.author: drewlevin
top_tweets.name: Drew Levin
top_tweets.summary_zh: PM 技巧：故意對產品品質發布不準確言論來激怒用戶社群，藉此快速辨識出系統問題並排定修復優先順序——這是作者活躍於社群的主要原因。

top_tweets.text: You cannot think your way to a perfect design. Only building and testing, over many iterations, can reveal the flaws in your mental model and provide the feedback you need to create the best design possible.
top_tweets.author: fchollet
top_tweets.name: François Chollet
top_tweets.summary_zh: François Chollet 強調無法靠純思考達成完美設計，唯有透過多輪建構與測試，才能揭露心智模型的盲點並獲得回饋，最終打磨出最佳設計。

top_tweets.text: honest question. if everyone’s using AI for design, won’t things start to look the same after a point?
top_tweets.author: Ahmedcreatives
top_tweets.name: Ahmed
top_tweets.summary_zh: 誠實提問：如果所有人都用 AI 做設計，產出最終會不會趨向同質化？留言討論踴躍，顯示業界對此現象的關切。

top_tweets.text:
Whether design belongs in Figma or Claude Design is a distraction from a bigger shift.

1️⃣ Design will become autonomous. More helpful to think of it as 𝙳𝙴𝚂𝙸𝙶𝙽.𝚖𝚍, used by your coding agents running your software factory.

2️⃣ Specialized “personal” design tools generated
top_tweets.author: rauchg
top_tweets.name: Guillermo Rauch
top_tweets.summary_zh: Guillermo Rauch 認為設計該在 Figma 還是 Claude Design 不是重點；真正轉變是設計將自主化為 DESIGN.md 供 coding agents 使用，並且會出現針對個人需求生成的專門設計工具。

top_tweets.text:
McKinsey presentation design lead says every consultant using ChatGPT or Claude to make decks.

Already seeing “fingerprints” of AI-created decks. Says “problem was never really design to begin with”. The real skill is “narrative” and AI decks won’t close that gap for long time. https://t.co/QznXJXywVs
top_tweets.author: bearlyai
top_tweets.name: Bearly AI
top_tweets.summary_zh: McKinsey 簡報設計主管觀察，所有顧問都在用 ChatGPT 或 Claude 做簡報，已能辨識 AI 痕跡；但問題核心從來不是設計本身，而是敘事能力——AI 短期內無法彌補這段差距。
